subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Stellar Nurseries Across the Galaxy
description: A quick, practical guide to Stellar Nurseries Across the Galaxy.

hook.text: The Milky Way forms about three new stars every year. Star formation happens mostly in the spiral arms.

buildup.text: Giant molecular clouds in the arms contain enough gas to birth thousands of stars over millions of years.

discovery.text: The Carina Nebula is one of the largest nurseries—hosting massive stars that will die as supernovae within millions of years.

twist.text: Starburst galaxies form stars 100 times faster than the Milky Way. They burn through gas in a cosmic frenzy.

climax.text: JWST is revealing nurseries in the earliest galaxies—stars forming just hundreds of millions of years after the Big Bang.

punchline.text: Three new stars a year in our galaxy alone. Creation never stops.

quiz.question: Where in the Milky Way does most star formation occur?
options: In the spiral arms, within giant molecular clouds
At the galactic center near the supermassive black hole
In the halo surrounding the galactic disk
